title: THERMAL_ANALYSIS
description: Thermal sensor data and short thermal-response test written by m09_thermal_health. Log glob: logs/thermal_health_*.json

report_type.description: Report type discriminator. Present in v2+ logs.

idle_snapshot.description: Passive thermal snapshot taken at idle (no load applied)

cpu_freq_mhz.description: CPU frequency observed during idle snapshot

thermal_response_test.description: Short thermal response test: brief CPU load applied; measures heat rise, throttle response, and recovery

performed.description: Whether the test was actually run (may be skipped if sensors unavailable or emergency stop triggered)

skipped_reason.description: Reason test was skipped, if applicable

idle_temp_c.description: Temperature immediately before load was applied (°C)

time_to_peak_seconds.description: Seconds from load start to peak temperature

recovery_temp_c.description: Temperature after a recovery wait period (°C)

recovery_time_seconds.description: Seconds from load end until temperature fell below idle + 5 °C (or recovery_temp_c was sampled)

cpu_freq_during_mhz.description: CPU frequency at peak load (throttling evidence)

throttling_detected.description: True if cpu_freq_during < cpu_freq_before by more than threshold

emergency_stop.description: True if test was aborted because temperature exceeded emergency threshold

confidence.description: Confidence in thermal response data quality

limitations.description: Caveats on test accuracy (e.g. live OS interference, short duration, single-core load only)

recommendations.description: Thermal-response-specific follow-up actions (e.g. clean heatsink, reapply thermal paste)

passive_verdict.description: Verdict based on idle/passive sensor readings only (no load test). Maps to the top-level 'verdict' field when no active test was run.

response_verdict.description: Verdict based on the active thermal response test. SKIPPED when the test was not performed (sensors unavailable, already too hot, emergency stop triggered before test started).

overall_thermal_verdict.description: Combined verdict considering both passive state and active response. Prefer this field for report generation. UNKNOWN when neither passive nor active data was available.

response_confidence.description: Confidence in the response_verdict. 'low' or 'unknown' when sensors are missing, the test was skipped, or when RescueZilla/Linux thermal behaviour may differ from OEM firmware behaviour under Windows.

response_limitations.description: Limitations of the active response test — e.g. 'test_skipped_no_sensors', 'single_core_load_only', 'live_linux_may_differ_from_windows_thermal_policy', 'uefi_thermal_management_not_observable_from_os'

generated.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (preferred over timestamp for new logs)

source_module.description: Module that wrote this log (e.g. m09_thermal_health)

interpretation.description: Report-facing interpretation block for explanation generation
